# Quantity of motion and mass

## Short Teaching Summary
Momentum is a physical quantity that includes both mass and velocity, characterizing the "quantity of motion" of an object.

## Difficulty
0.00

## Prerequisites
- Object motion has momentum
- Momentum unit standard
- Momentum direction equals velocity
- Large momentum condition
- Object motion quantity

## Related Concepts
- Object motion has momentum (requires)
- Momentum unit standard (requires)
- Momentum direction equals velocity (requires)
- Large momentum condition (requires)
- Object motion quantity (requires)

## Misconceptions
- None recorded.

## Best Examples
- The momentum of an object is equal to the mass of the object times the velocity of the object (p = m • v).
- Momentum is directly proportional to an object's mass and directly proportional to the object's velocity.
- Momentum is related to topics like Newton's Laws, Vectors, and Motion and Forces in Two Dimensions.

## Best Analogy
- Unlike kinetic energy, momentum depends equally on an object’s mass and velocity.
